text.tspan: TileLink interface, registers, interrupts and alerts
Single-port SRAM implements all queues and buffers:
Controller-side: Command Queue, TX & Rx Buffers, Response Queue, and IBI
Target-side: TX Buffers & Rx Buffer, Tx Descs & Rx Desc, Async Events and IBI
SCL is push-pull driven or tri-stated. SDA may be push-pull, open drain or tri-stated.
Most of the design operates on the IP clock. A 50MHz clock lends itself naturally to
Single Data Rate (SDR) signaling at 12.5MHz, with Double Data Rate (HDR-DDR) offering 25Mbps.
The Target Transceiver logic operates directly on SCL to avoid CDC delays,
and the Target Reset Detector does the same to reduce power consumption in Deep Sleep.
